title: Port C++ external scanner and grammar
description:
Generate the C++ grammar package and port its external scanner to Go.

The C++ external scanner is ~2000 lines — the most complex scanner in the target language set. It handles raw string literals, template disambiguation, and other context-sensitive C++ syntax. This is separated from the other grammar ports because of its exceptional complexity.

If the manual port proves impractical, the CGo fallback escape hatch (wrapping the C scanner behind the Go ExternalScanner interface) is an acceptable outcome for v1.0. Document the decision either way.

Deliverables:
- Generate C++ grammar using tsgo-generate
- Port scanner.cc (~2000 lines) to Go ExternalScanner implementation, OR document why CGo fallback is used
- If CGo fallback: implement the adapter wrapping C scanner behind Go interface (test-only CGo dependency, not in the main runtime)

Tests:
- Parse real C++ files from popular repos (LLVM, Chromium, etc.)
- Differential test against C tree-sitter
- Stress test complex C++ features (templates, raw strings, nested namespaces)

Reference: docs/design.md §4 Strategy 3: CGo fallback, tree-sitter-cpp src/scanner.cc
